text:
{
  "title": "A Guide to Agent-native Product Management",
  "tags": ["AI产品管理", "Agent", "Compound-Engineering", "产品策略", "LLM"],
  "linked_concepts": [
    "agent-native-product-management",
    "compound-engineering",
    "对话即工作",
    "product-strategy",
    "product-pulse",
    "crossing-the-chasm",
    "good-strategy-bad-strategy",
    "smart",
    "sdlc",
    "target-problem",
    "8020",
    "strategy-document",
    "approach"
  ],
  "dimensions": {
    "one_liner": "Agent-native PM treats the conversation as the work — PMs collaborate with LLM agents to compress what used to be hours of analysis into minutes, freeing them to focus on strategy over execution.",
    "explanation": {
      "what": "Agent-native Product Management is a way of doing PM work where most interdisciplinary tasks (data analysis, ticket writing, user-feedback synthesis, SQL queries) happen through natural-language conversation with an LLM agent like Claude Code, rather than across 100+ disconnected SaaS tools.",
      "why": "Modern PMs face software overload: the average company runs 100+ subscriptions, and PMs sit at the cross-functional intersection of all of them. Conversation-driven workflows collapse that surface area, restore focus to strategic decisions, and turn 3-hour investigations into minutes.",
      "how": "The workflow loops Plan → Ship → Review through a Compound Engineering framework. Plan: a quarterly strategy document drives `/ce-strategy` interviews and feature specs. Ship: agents implement and deploy. Review: a `product-pulse` skill collects learnings and metrics, which feed back into the next strategy iteration."
    },
    "details": [
      "PMs no longer write tickets by hand — they describe outcomes in conversation and let agents draft the tickets.",
      "Three-hour analytics investigations now resolve in a single chat exchange, often a few minutes.",
      "The author runs all PM work through Claude Code; the conversation IS the deliverable.",
      "Strategy is revisited quarterly; feature work is reviewed fortnightly via product-pulse metrics.",
      "Compound Engineering treats Plan/Ship/Review as a self-reinforcing loop: every shipped feature is an experiment that updates strategy."
    ],
    "structure": "Plan → Ship → Review → (learnings update strategy) → Plan ...",
    "actions": [
      "Short-term: pick one weekly PM task (e.g. analytics investigation) and try it conversation-first with Claude.",
      "Medium-term: write a one-page strategy doc and run quarterly /ce-strategy reviews against it.",
      "Long-term: set up a product-pulse metric review cadence so learnings feed back into strategy iteration."
    ],
    "linked_concepts": [
      "agent-native-product-management",
      "compound-engineering",
      "对话即工作",
      "product-strategy",
      "product-pulse",
      "crossing-the-chasm",
      "good-strategy-bad-strategy",
      "smart",
      "sdlc",
      "target-problem",
      "8020",
      "strategy-document",
      "approach"
    ]
  }
}
